health_zone_table.basis: SitRep54 Table 2 (2026-07-07) transcribed + reconciled against the INSP PDF (post 25108, media 25109, pdf sha256 a8086420e34653d8803d7dd6371f5de83f23a7a526be8ceca61987edd932abbb, 13 pages, 1040438 bytes), byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian visual receipt.

reconciliation.note: Rows reconcile to official province/national totals: 1742 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified = 1759; all 600 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unattributed context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate: Ituri 147 analyzed / 45 positive (30.6%); Nord-Kivu 90 analyzed / 6 positive (6.7%); Sud-Kivu 3 collected and under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 51 positives over 237 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep54 Tableau 7: 750 in isolation at end of day (232 confirmed / 518 suspected) at 94% global bed occupancy; Nord-Kivu at 137.0% saturation, Ituri 85.0%, Sud-Kivu 86.6%. Exits over 24h: 5 recoveries (all Ituri), 16 deaths (suspect/confirmed), 32 non-cases, 9 escapes (all Ituri); 118 admissions and 0 HGR transfers.
The summary sentence reuses the J-1 census of 694 ('694 malades ... dont 232 confirmes et 518 suspects'); the Tableau-7 end-of-day census is 750 (232 + 518).
highlights: 51 new confirmed cases and 20 deaths (55% community) were reported for 2026-07-07, in Ituri (45 cases, 20 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (6 cases, 0 deaths). Confirmed cases rose to 1759 and deaths to 600; the banner CFR is 34.1%. The headline is flagged 'Donnees reactualisees' (DHIS2 base cleaning/harmonization).

No newly affected health zone this cycle; affected zones hold at 37/104. Two PCR RADIONE-positive cases were reported in Kisangani (Tshopo) but are EXCLUDED from totals pending RT-PCR (Altona) validation; one is linked to Nia-Nia.
The provider strike continues in Bunia and Rwampara (Ituri). Nord-Kivu CTE occupancy is 137% and global bed occupancy is 94% (750 in isolation).

Ituri concentrates 91.0% of cases (n=1601) and 85.2% of deaths (n=511), CFR 31.9%. Bunia (483), Rwampara (364), Mongbwalu (310), Nyankunde (97) and Nizi (94) remain the most affected zones and hold ~84.2% of provincial cases; Nizi is progressing faster than other zones.
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial CFR at 56.8%, led by Katwa (67.2%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (65.5%). Sud-Kivu remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death (no new case since 2026-05-26).
laboratory: SitRep54 reports Ituri collected/analyzed 147 samples with 45 positive (30.6%).
Nord-Kivu collected/analyzed 90 samples with 6 positive (6.7%). Sud-Kivu collected 3 samples under analysis. National: 51 positive over 237 analyzed (21.5%).
publication_context: INSP SitRep 54 reports through 2026-07-07 (post 25108; WP publish 2026-07-08T22:21:34Z; PDF media 25109). The report date is 07 July 2026; publication date 08 July 2026.
SitRep54 is the current/latest operational snapshot; the cumulative rose by 51 confirmed and 20 deaths over 2026-07-07 vs SitRep53.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 37 of 104 health zones; no newly affected zone this cycle.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1601 of 1759 DRC confirmed cases (91.0%) and 511 deaths (CFR 31.9%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1220 alerts of the day were reported (49 J-1 + 1171 new) and 970 investigated (79.5%); Ituri 44.7% (202/452), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (759/759), Sud-Kivu 100.0% (9/9). The PDF renders the investigated total as '9 70' (spacing); the narrative instead states 1232 alerts and 78.7% investigated.
304 suspected cases of the day were validated per Tableau 3 (212 living, 92 community deaths); the narrative says 306 (214 living + 92 deaths). Ituri 202, Nord-Kivu 99, Sud-Kivu 3.

14119 contacts were under follow-up and 11300 seen (80.0%); Ituri 78.9% (completeness 78.1%; ZS Lita, Damas, Rimba, Kambala, Logo, Fataki, Mandima had no data at consolidation), Nord-Kivu 85.4%. 643 new contacts listed (Ituri 571, Nord-Kivu 72); 4 became suspect (all Ituri); 530 completed 21-day follow-up.
At PoE/PoC, 4 dead bodies were intercepted (Ituri 2, Nord-Kivu 2) and all swabbed (100%); 156344 persons passed through PoE/PoC this cycle.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 49; new alerts 1171; total alerts of the day 1220. Tableau 3 investigated 970 (79.5%), printed '9 70' (spacing). The narrative instead states 1232 alerts, 1232 investigated at 78.7%, and 306 validated suspects (214 living + 92 deaths); table values (1220 / 970 / 304) are promoted, narrative retained as conflict.

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation at 137.0%; national isolation occupancy is 94% (750 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 80.0% versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting/investigation in Ituri (44.7% investigation rate) risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk among health workers.
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 56.8% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; two PCR RADIONE-positive Kisangani (Tshopo) cases are under RT-PCR validation and 4 dead bodies were intercepted at PoE/PoC this cycle.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 4 (07 July): 14119 under follow-up, 11300 seen, 80.0%. Ituri completeness 78.1%; ZS Lita, Damas, Rimba, Kambala, Logo, Fataki, Mandima had no contact data at consolidation. Tableau 5: 643 new contacts listed (Ituri 571, Nord-Kivu 72), 4 became suspect (all Ituri), 530 completed 21-day follow-up.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep54 Tableau 7 end-of-day census 750 at 94% occupancy (Ituri 518 @85.0%, Nord-Kivu 193 @137.0%, Sud-Kivu 39 @86.6%). Census 750 = 232 confirmed + 518 suspected; province rows 518+193+39=750 concordant. The summary sentence reuses the J-1 bed count 694; end-of-day is 750. Exits: 5 recoveries (all Ituri), 16 deaths, 32 non-cases, 9 escapes (all Ituri); 118 admissions, 0 HGR transfers.

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia and validate the two Kisangani/Tshopo PCR RADIONE-positive cases before any count-bearing use
Relieve isolation load at 94% national occupancy and Nord-Kivu 137% saturation (56.8% provincial CFR); close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction/expansion
Improve contact follow-up (80.0% vs 95% target), raise Ituri alert investigation above 44.7%, reinforce intervention teams in affected ZS, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog

preserved_conflicts: SitRep54 headline is DRC-only (1759 confirmed / 600 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope snapshot adds the Uganda component (20/2/11) separately.

CONFLICT (preserved): SitRep54 alert totals — Tableau 3 shows 1220 alerts of the day and 970 investigated (printed '9 70'), while the narrative says 1232 alerts and 1232 investigated at 78.7%. The promoted values follow the table (1220 alerts / 970 investigated / 79.5%).

CONFLICT (preserved): validated suspects — Tableau 3 totals 304 suspected cases of the day (212 living + 92 deaths), while the narrative says 306 validated suspects (214 living + 92 deaths). The promoted daily-suspect count is 304.

CONFLICT (preserved): SitRep54 Tableau 7 end-of-day isolation census is 750 (232 confirmed + 518 suspected; province rows Ituri 518 + Nord-Kivu 193 + Sud-Kivu 39 = 750) at 94% occupancy, but the summary sentence says '694 malades ... dont 232 confirmes et 518 suspects' (694 is the J-1 bed count; 232+518=750). The promoted census is 750.
SitRep54 supersedes SitRep53 (single report-day advance): +51 confirmed and +20 confirmed deaths for 2026-07-07. Ituri +45 (20 deaths), Nord-Kivu +6 (0 deaths), Sud-Kivu +0.
SitRep54 carries an Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone this cycle; the affected-zone count holds at 37 of 104 (same zone set as SitRep53).

Two PCR RADIONE-positive cases in Kisangani (Tshopo) are reported but EXCLUDED from SitRep54 totals pending RT-PCR (Altona) validation and field investigation; one is linked to Nia-Nia, the other has no apparent geographic link outside Kisangani. They are carried on the existing suspected-watch overlay, not as counted cases (sourceConflict key sr54_kisangani_tshopo_suspected_watch).

The SitRep54 PDF is labeled 'Draft2_...' but is attached to the official INSP SR54 post (25108, media 25109) and used as the official receipt unless INSP supersedes it. The banner flags 'Donnees reactualisees' (DHIS2 base cleaning/harmonization).

Uganda anchor carried at 20 cumulative confirmed / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (Uganda MoH, last reviewed 2026-06-21), so country-scope is 1779 confirmed / 602 deaths / 296 recovered. NOTE: the Uganda EVD dashboard (evd-daily.health.go.ug) on 2026-07-08 now shows 17 recoveries; the recovered anchor is deliberately held at 11 for series consistency pending a formal re-anchor decision, matching SitRep53's treatment.
Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death (Miti-Murhesa).
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1, Boga 1/1); unstable small-number ratios.

review_notes: SitRep #054 (report date 2026-07-07; INSP post 25108; media 25109; WP publish 2026-07-08T22:21:34Z). PDF sha256 a8086420e34653d8803d7dd6371f5de83f23a7a526be8ceca61987edd932abbb, 13 pages, 1040438 bytes, byte-verified against the live INSP upload and the source-librarian visual receipt.

SitRep54 supersedes SitRep53 (single report-day advance). DRC headline 1759 confirmed / 600 confirmed deaths (CFR 34.1%); +51 confirmed and +20 confirmed deaths vs SitRep53. On 07 July, 51 new confirmed (Ituri 45, Nord-Kivu 6) carried 55% community deaths.

No newly affected health zone this cycle; affected-zone count holds at 37 of 104 (same zone set as SitRep53). Health-zone rows reconcile to 1759 confirmed / 600 deaths: 1742 named confirmed plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation; all 600 deaths named-zone attributed.

Two PCR RADIONE-positive cases were reported in Kisangani (Tshopo), EXCLUDED from totals pending RT-PCR (Altona) validation; one linked to Nia-Nia. Carried on the existing suspected-watch overlay, not as counted cases.

Tableau 7 end-of-day census 750 (232 confirmed / 518 suspected) at 94% occupancy; Nord-Kivu 137.0% saturation. Recovered advanced to 285 DRC cumulative. The summary sentence reuses the J-1 bed count 694 (232+518=750).
Laboratory: Ituri 147 analyzed / 45 positive (30.6%); Nord-Kivu 90 analyzed / 6 positive (6.7%); Sud-Kivu 3 under analysis (237 analyzed / 51 positive overall, 21.5%).

Uganda anchor carried at 20 / 2 / 11 (last reviewed 2026-06-21); country-scope 1779 / 602 / 296. The Uganda dashboard now shows 17 recoveries; the anchor is held at 11 for series consistency pending a formal re-anchor. Provider strike continues in Bunia and Rwampara; funding gap ~USD 20M.
